coreConcept: The water cycle is the way water moves around Earth, changing between liquid, gas, and sometimes ice. Sunlight helps water evaporate, clouds form, and then water falls back to the ground, so the same water keeps moving in different ways.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun warms water so it can evaporate into the air.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Atmosphere
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Holder and transporter of water vapor
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The air around Earth holds water vapor and moves it so clouds can form.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Bodies of Water
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Oceans, lakes, and rivers are where much water evaporates from.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Storage and change of vapor to droplets
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds are made of tiny water droplets or ice that form when water vapor cools.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Plants
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Plants release water through transpiration, sending moisture from leaves into the atmosphere.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation and Transpiration
storyOfProcess.description: Sun heats water in oceans, lakes, soil, and plants; water turns into vapor and rises into the air.

storyOfProcess.name: Cooling and Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: Warm water vapor rises and cools in the atmosphere, turning into tiny droplets that form clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When droplets join and grow heavy, they fall as rain, snow, sleet, or hail back to Earth.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection and Runoff
storyOfProcess.description: Water collects in rivers, lakes, and oceans or soaks into the ground; it may flow back to big bodies of water and the cycle starts again.

corePrinciples.name: Conservation of Water
corePrinciples.description: Water continually moves and changes form, but the total amount stays the same on Earth, so water is reused.

corePrinciples.name: Energy Drives the Cycle
corePrinciples.description: The Sun's energy powers evaporation and moves water through the cycle, so sunlight controls many changes.

corePrinciples.name: Phase Changes Matter
corePrinciples.description: Water changes between liquid, gas, and solid; these changes (evaporation, condensation, freezing) make the cycle work.
